title: The Giant Rescue on Volcano Island
summary: When a frantic letter arrives from a small purple dragonet named Dimitar, the adventurer Seeker and his companions, Hattie the Mouse and Izzy, set off for a mysterious circular island. They soon discover that Dimitar’s castle is built on the slopes of a rumbling volcano that is about to erupt. Using a magic flying ladder and an ancient "Embiggening" spell, Seeker transforms into a giant to physically move the stone fortress to safety before the island’s tiny dragonet inhabitants lose their home.

highlightQuote.text: Well, my hands are butterfinger right now, but in the mornings when I wake up, they won't be butterfingers anymore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Let's tell a Seeker story.
Ding dong Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker says, "Who's there?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi Hattie. I'm glad you just got here because I need your help with something. I just got a very interesting letter and I have to decide what to do. You want to read it?
And so Seeker handed Hattie a piece of purple paper with some writing on it.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: But she didn't know how to read yet.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Seeker said, "Oh, sorry. I know how to read. I'll read it to you." It says, "Dear old friend"—and Seeker says, "That's me, I'm an old friend of this whoever's writing this letter"—"I need your help. Please come immediately, as quickly as you possibly can, because I have many problems that I need your help solving. Me and all of my dragonet friends are in a real pickle. Faithfully, your old friend Dimitar."
Now what do you think of that, Hattie?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh, that's good. And Hattie said, "Could I come with you?"
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, yes, it sounds like Dimitar really needs help. Do you know who Dimitar is?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I do.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dimitar is indeed my old friend, and he's a very silly creature. He is a dragon, but he's something called a dragonet. They are very small, and Dimitar himself is a lovely shade of purple. And nowadays he lives in an amazing place. It's an island. You know what an island is, don't you, Hattie?
Uh-huh.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: It's land surrounded by water, and this particular land is perfectly circular. And I think that whatever the problem is that Dimitar has, we might want to be careful on our way there. So, I have a potion that can take us there, but I think we should bring something that helps us fly. Do you know what I have that can help with that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A skyship.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're right. I do have a skyship named Vendrasherna, but I think for this mission we should take another way to fly that's a lot smaller.
A magic ladder.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: That's correct. So Seeker got his magic ladder and—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but do you know how to get there?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I happen to have a teleportation potion to take us—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but do you always have going-home potions?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yes. And Seeker opened his cloak to show a pocket labeled "Going Home Potions" that was full of lots of little going-home potions. And Seeker said, "You see, I'm always prepared. I never run out. We're always going to be able to get home just in case we need to." But here, I've been digging around in my potion closet. I haven't been to this particular place in a long time, but it's—you see, it's a circular bottle, just like this circular island. I don't remember what it tastes like, but let's try it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Seeker sipped the potion, and then the ladder also somehow sipped it, even though it didn't have a mouth, glug, glug, glug, and he offered it to Hattie. Did Hattie take a sip?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mm-hm. And she said, "Kajouska, Kamouska, Kamousie. Jouska, Kajouska, Kajousie mouse. Jouska, Kamouska, Kamousie."
Kamousie!
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: And so they all said the rhyme together, except the ladder, who said the rhyme in ladder language, and then pring, poof, they appeared flying through the sky. And it was a good thing they had brought their magical flying ladder, because both Seeker and Hattie were holding on to the rungs of their flying magic ladder, which was soaring through the clouds. And you know what they could see far below them?
What?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: The beautiful sparkling ocean. It was blue, and the waves had little white caps on them, and the waves were rolling in towards the beach at the shore of an island that was a beautiful circle shape. And in the middle of the island was one big pointy mountain. And Seeker said, "Okay, let's get a little closer."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then you can land.
Well, we'll fly around a little bit and see what we can see, but then we'll pick a place to land.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, yeah, but let's land on a shady spot. I see a shady spot right over there where there's not much. So you can land there. A perfect place.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, good idea. And so the ladder dove down from high in the clouds, and they could hear the wind whistling "whoosh," and they could feel the wind blowing on their faces like this. And they dove straight down closer and closer to the ocean, and then zoomed ahead to the edge of the island where there was a bright yellow sparkling beach, and then the shady area that Hattie had spotted. And what made it shady?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Because there was lots of trees.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yeah, but let's look out for our friend.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, yes, we'll see if we can see Dimitar or any other dragonets. But shooo, the ladder brought them in for a landing under the shady trees. And suddenly, all was quiet. And Seeker said, "Okay, let's just look around a little bit and maybe listen and sniff and see if we can hear or see or smell anything." What do you notice, Hattie? Anything interesting?
No. Not yet.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Do you hear anything?
Uh-huh. I hear some big footsteps.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Big footsteps! What do they sound like?
They sound like Dimitar. Crunch, crunch, crunch. What's up with you, Dimitar?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "You know, it's funny that those footsteps would sound like a big creature, because Dimitar's actually very small." But indeed, Hattie was correct. The "crunch, crunch, crunch" was the sound of a tiny dragonet pretending to be a big dragon by making mouth noises as it walked. And it turned the corner around a tree and said "crunch, crunch, crunch" with its mouth and looked at Seeker and Hattie very meanly. "Who are you?" it said.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We're Hattie, Seeker, and Izzy the kiddo who's pretending to be Mama Izzy.
Oh, I never heard of you. What are you doing here?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: We're looking for our new friend Dimitar because he has the big problem because he's very mischievous.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Dimitar? Well—and suddenly this dragonet, who I forgot to mention was a blue and green one, his whole attitude changed. He had been looking mean and trying to make himself sound really big and scary, but as soon as Hattie mentioned Dimitar, he looked happy. He said, "Oh, Dimitar! Well, I'm glad you mentioned—"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi Dimitar!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, I'm not Dimitar. I'm Dimitar's friend. What was this one's name?
Pouska.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I'm Pouska. I'm Dimitar's assistant. That means I help him out with everything. And boy does he need a lot of help with everything. Do you want me to take you to him?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Mm-hm.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, Dimitar lives high up on the side of the mountain in his little castle. I can just fly there, but it looks like you don't have wings.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but Seeker has his magic ladder.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, a ladder's good for like climbing trees and stuff. I don't see how that—but then Seeker made the ladder lift up into the air and fly around a little bit and zoom around in a circle. And Pouska said, "Oh, I gotcha. Okay, let's go. Follow me." And Pouska flapped his little blue and green dragonet wings and flew straight up in the air. And Hattie and Seeker and also Mama Izzy—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

—grabbed onto the ladder and they all flew straight up, following the dragonet. And they flapped up into the air—or zoomed up in the case of the ladder—up over the shady trees where they'd been talking. And now they could see the rest of the circular island flying beneath them as they zoomed towards the middle of the island and the mountain where Dimitar lives. And they flew over lots of forests of trees and little rivers. And then they started to see little houses. And you know who lived in those little houses?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Dragonets! Tiny dragons. And they could see them playing in the air, flapping around and chasing each other and laughing. And also in their yard, sitting in chairs, sipping on drinks, or working in their gardens, growing things to eat. And there were lots of little dragonets of all different colors living on this island.
Yeah, yeah, but what do you guys eat, Dimitar?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And so—well, we're not at Dimitar yet. They arrived at Dimitar's little castle, high up on the side of the mountain, in the middle of the circular island. And they landed in front of the door. And Pouska said, "Okay, this is as far as I take you. Good luck with Dimitar, ha-ha!" And he flew off. And so the ladder, with Seeker and Hattie and Izzy landed. And there was in front of them a very big wooden door in the middle of the castle.
Yeah, but where's the doorbell?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Ah, good question, said Seeker. Let's look around.
Yeah, but then—but then Hattie the Mouse spotted a big round doorbell.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, there it is! You want to press it?
Yeah. And then I pressed it. Ding dong!
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And it made a ding-dong sound, but it was a bigger, louder ding-dong than any of them had ever heard because it came from a giant bell high up in a tower at the top of Dimitar's castle.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong! Bing bong, bing bong, bing bong! Bing bong, bing bong!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Wow, I like the sound of that. Let's press it again, ha-ha!" And he pressed the button again and it went "bing bong, bing bong." And suddenly there was a "Hey!" and the wooden doors pushed outward all of a sudden, "pshhh," and a little tiny purple dragonet popped out.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi dragonet!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who's making all that racket with my doorbell?
It's just us.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Who are you?
We're Seeker, Hattie, and Mama Izzy.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness, Seeker! Now you know Seeker's an old friend of mine. "Oh, hello Dimitar," said Seeker. "It's so good to see you."
It's so good to see you too. I've got a new baby at home resting, who's very new, that has on a new name called Baby James.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Dimitar said, "Oh my, is Baby James a dragonet by any chance?"
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No, he's a mouse.
A mouse! Very interesting. Well, Baby James would be welcome here on my island anytime. But you know, right now might not be the best time to visit because, boy, have we got some trouble.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but what's your problem?
Well, I'm glad you all came here to help me out. Now what are your names again?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Hattie, Seeker, and Isabella the kiddo who's pretending to be Mama Izzy.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Isabella the kiddo who's pretending to be Mama Izzy. Hello and welcome, and to you Hattie the mouse as well, and come on in Seeker. And who's this over here?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: It's—it's Isabella who's—
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: No, I mean this tall thing made of rope and wood.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "Oh, that's the ladder. The ladder's our very important and faithful friend who can fly. He doesn't really speak very much, but he's actually quite smart if you get to know him." And the ladder took a bow, leaning over to say hello to Dimitar. And Dimitar said, "Well come on in, come on in." And so everyone went inside the little castle. And they sat around a little table, and Dimitar snapped one of his claws like this—snap, snap—and two of his friends that also lived in the castle with him, who were orange dragonets, zipped right into the kitchen and zipped back out with a bunch of snacks. And what were the dragonet snacks?
Dimitar, what are the dragonet—what are the dragonet snacks?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Dimitar said, "Oh yes, these are some little bowls full of delicious crunchy things that we dragonets like to eat. They're actually dried fish. Have you ever had dried fish?"
No.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: It's quite delicious, and a little salty. Make sure you get some water too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but I only had like things that people eat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, people food. We haven't had people food around here in a long time. I hope you're not too hungry. And then Dimitar said, "Pardon me for a moment," and he stuck his whole face into a bowl of dried crunchy fish and crunch, crunch, crunch. Ah! Excuse me. Now then, let's get down to business. You may have noticed that my little castle right in the center of this circular island is sitting on the side of a mountain. Well, you'd be wrong about that.
And Seeker sort of laughed. "I think I know what's coming here."
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: What?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Dimitar said, "Well it looks like a mountain, but it's not. We've recently found out—" and Seeker said, "Oh, I knew all along." And Dimitar said, "It's really a volcano." And just at that moment, coincidentally, the whole floor started to shake and it was a shaking just like this. And Dimitar slapped the table and said, "Gosh darn it!" And the picture fell off the wall and went "clonk" right down to the floor. And Dimitar said, "Oh, that was one of my favorite pictures of me. Could somebody put it back up, please?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then I put it back up and held it there very tightly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Dimitar said, "Oh, thanks." And he snapped his claw again—snap, snap—and one of the orange dragonets came with a little tiny hammer and hammered it back into the wall so it wouldn't fall down. And Dimitar said, "Anyway, as I was saying, we've got a little problem because this volcano, it might erupt anytime, and we don't know what's going on with it. And so we need somebody to go and have a look." And Seeker said, "Wait a minute, have a look? What do you mean? Like fly right down into the crater in the middle? That sounds like a dangerous job." And Dimitar said, "Well—"
Well, I could just—I'm good at holding things, so I can move your castle down from the volcano onto the mountain so you would be safer.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Dimitar said, "My goodness, that solution to the problem would not have occurred to me. And how do you propose to do this?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, my hands are butterfinger right now, but in the mornings when I wake up, they won't be butterfingers anymore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Aha. And are you saying that you personally, you yourself, Izzy the kiddo, could lift my entire castle down the mountain?
Duh, yeah.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: But are you much bigger than you seem? Because you're inside my castle now, it's much bigger than you. But if you could magically become much bigger, then maybe you could lift the castle down the mountain.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I'm really big. Yeah, I'm way too big to just, like, like jump, yeah, yeah, yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "You know, I have an idea. I happen to have a spell in my little spellbook right here." And he opens up his pocket of his cloak and he rummages around a little bit and he pulls out a magical little book that you've never seen before. And it has very strange writing all over it. And when he pops open the book, it makes a little ringing sound like that. And a yellow glow comes out of the pages and he says, "Yes, yes, this is a precious ancient spellbook. And in it is a very powerful spell called Embiggen. Do you know what embiggening is?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's making someone very, very big, but just for a minute or two. It doesn't last very long. What do you think, Izzy? If we embiggen you with my magic, maybe you could move the castle to safety.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, well, if Seeker does that, maybe he—Seeker, would you do that? I'm—I have butterfingers.
Oh, you have butterfingers. What about you, Hattie? Do you think we could embiggen you instead?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No. But if Seeker—do you mind getting embiggened?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, "All right. I've been embiggened before and I know how to do it. But I think for safety, everyone should get out of the castle first and then we can move it with me embiggened. And I think also—"
Yeah, so then I and me and my friends, but not James, but I stepped out.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Okay, so everyone stepped out of the castle and Dimitar snapped his claws once again and whistled loudly and shrilly and said, "Hey, everybody out! Everybody out! Come on, pronto, pronto!"
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And then, and then everybody came out.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And all—dozens of little dragonets of all different colors, some of whom obviously had just been waking up—woken up from a nap and were not happy about it, flapped right out of the castle and some of them still had little bits of food in their mouths, little fishes flapping around and hanging out. And they flopped out of the castle and said, "What's going on here?" And Dimitar said, "Pipe down, everybody. Hold still. We're gonna all go over here in this open field next to the castle and I'll explain. Seeker here is going to get very big somehow and well, I guess he's going to move our castle somewhere else."

And at this, all the little dragonets were very upset. They said, "What? What? What are you talking about? Move the castle? The castle's here, it's never moving anywhere, it's a castle made of stone, that's ridiculous. Why would we need to do that anyway? Oh yeah, we do need to do that anyway because the volcano might erupt. Okay, let's do it."
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And so once the dragonets had gotten that out of their system, Seeker calmly sat himself down and crossed his legs and said, "Okay Hattie, okay Izzy, come on over and hold my hands. And that way when I embiggen, I'll have you in my hands and then you can come with me to help out just in case I need help, okay? You'll be small but I'll be big." And so he began to read the embiggening spell. "Embiggening, embiggening, I'm gonna get real big, real big, embiggening, embiggening." And more and more of that. And there were a lot of words in the spell and after a long time it was finally done.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And then there was a very strange sound like this. And Seeker, they looked up, all of a sudden, was gigantic! He was three times as tall as the entire castle, including the tall bell tower with the very big bells that they had rung. And Izzy and Hattie looked around and they found themselves in a dark place that looked like the inside of a giant hand. And suddenly the fingers opened and Seeker said, "Oh, hello! You made it."
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: And so Izzy and Hattie were standing on Seeker's very big hand and Seeker looked around and said, "Okay, I have to be very careful where I put my feet. I don't want to step on any tiny dragonets." And then his feet made a boom, boom, boom as he stepped toward the castle. He said, "Okay, now one thing I need your help with, Izzy and Hattie, is deciding where to put the castle."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, we can just like, um, like, um—well, I'm too tired of this. Let's tell a different story. I guess we should be done with Seeker's stories because I'm too tired.
Oh, I'm sorry, Iz. And so, well let's just finish the story another time.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Okay. Okay, let's finish it another time.
Okay.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

characters.name: Pouska
characters.description: Dimitar's blue and green dragonet assistant who guides Seeker's group to the castle.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Baby James
characters.description: A new baby mouse mentioned in the story who stays at home.

characters.name: Magic Ladder
characters.description: A faithful, flying, and surprisingly smart ladder made of rope and wood.

characters.name: Orange Dragonets
characters.description: Dimitar's castle assistants who serve snacks and help around the castle.

places.name: Circular Island
places.description: A perfectly circular landmass rising from the waters of the distant reaches, this remote sanctuary serves as both the private residence of Dimitar and the hallowed grounds of the school.

places.name: The Volcano
places.description: A large mountain in the center of the circular island that is threatening to erupt.

places.name: Dimitar's Castle
places.description: Perched upon the scorched crags of the volcano, the weathered stone fortress where Dimitar and Hattie reside is dominated by a massive bell tower that echoes through the rising sulfurous mists.

places.name: The Shady Spot
places.description: A wooded area on the edge of the island where Seeker and Hattie land their magic ladder.

places.name: The Beach
places.description: The circular island is ringed by a brilliant, sparkling shore of lemon-yellow sand, providing a warm sanctuary for weary adventurers to dry off and recover from their journey through the depths.

places.name: Vindristjarna
places.description: Seeker's skyship, mentioned as an alternative way to travel.
